summary: Large fighter cleanup PR that replaced many unnamed FtMoveId enum values and motion-state table entries with semantic move IDs, introduced a MotionFlags typedef for fighter state-change/motion flag constants, and simplified symbol replacement tooling to call melee-replace-symbols. The diff is mostly mechanical renaming across common fighter data and per-character init/forward headers, with no PR body or review comments available.
what_changed: Renamed the leading FtMoveId enum entries in src/melee/ft/forward.h from generic unknown names to semantic names such as FtMoveId_None, FtMoveId_Default, FtMoveId_Attack12, FtMoveId_Attack13, FtMoveId_AttackDash, FtMoveId_AttackS3, and FtMoveId_AttackHi3.

Named many later FtMoveId enum entries for Kirby-copy neutral specials, item swings, throws, cliff attacks, and item actions, including FtMoveId_KbSpecialNMr through character-specific variants, FtMoveId_SwordSwing1/3/4/Dash, FtMoveId_BatSwing1/3/4/Dash, FtMoveId_ParasolSwing1/3/4/Dash, FtMoveId_HarisenSwing1/3/4/Dash, FtMoveId_StarRodSwing1/3/4/Dash, FtMoveId_LipstickSwing1/3/4/Dash, FtMoveId_LGunShoot, FtMoveId_FireFlowerShoot, FtMoveId_Screw, FtMoveId_ScopeRapid, FtMoveId_ScopeFire, FtMoveId_Hammer, and FtMoveId_WarpStarFall.
Updated hundreds of MotionState table entries in ftdata3.c and per-character ft*_Init.c files from FtMoveId_Unk01 << 24 to FtMoveId_Default << 24 where the move ID was the default/non-special value.
Changed motion-state move ID packing from multiplication by 1 << 24 to the clearer form FtMoveId_* << 24 throughout character init tables.
Added typedef u32 MotionFlags in src/melee/ft/forward.h and converted state-change flag constants from static u32 const to static MotionFlags const across fighter forward headers.

Replaced several Captain-specific item swing motion flag definitions in ftCaptain/forward.h with common ftCo_MF_* item swing flag constants, e.g. ftCo_MF_SwordSwing4 with FtMoveId_SwordSwing4 in ftCa_Init.c.
Simplified tools/functions.sh replace_symbol and reverse_symbol to pipe old:new pairs to melee-replace-symbols instead of locally hand-escaping names and running sed over source/asm/make/docs files.
Touched 48 files with roughly balanced additions and deletions, indicating a broad but mostly naming-preserving cleanup rather than new gameplay code.
smart_moves: Centralizing the type of motion/state-change bitmasks as MotionFlags makes intent clearer than raw u32 while preserving the underlying representation.
Using FtMoveId_Default instead of FtMoveId_Unk01 communicates that many common/death/appeal/zair-style states intentionally use the default move ID rather than an unknown value.
Replacing FtMoveId_* * (1 << 24) with FtMoveId_* << 24 directly documents that the move ID occupies the high byte/field of the MotionState value.
Reusing common ftCo_MF_* item swing flag constants avoids duplicating equivalent per-character flag definitions, shown in Captain's item swing cleanup.
The tooling change to melee-replace-symbols reduces bespoke shell escaping logic and likely makes future large symbol renames safer and more consistent.
decomp_lessons: When an integer field is repeatedly shifted into a table slot, naming the enum and using shift syntax can document the bit layout without changing table shape.
Prefer semantic defaults like FtMoveId_Default over Unk names once repeated usage patterns make the meaning clear; this improves future table audits.
Introduce narrow typedefs such as MotionFlags for repeated bitmask domains even when the ABI remains u32; it improves header readability and reduces accidental mixing of unrelated integers.
Large motion-state rename passes should update the shared enum and every affected character table in one PR to avoid partial naming drift.
Common fighter flags should be reused across characters where possible; local per-character aliases can obscure identical state-change behavior.
naming_conventions: Fighter move IDs use the FtMoveId_ prefix.
Generic non-specific move ID was renamed from FtMoveId_Unk01 to FtMoveId_Default; zero became FtMoveId_None.
Standard attacks use move-state-like names such as FtMoveId_Attack11, FtMoveId_Attack12, FtMoveId_Attack13, FtMoveId_AttackDash, FtMoveId_AttackS3, FtMoveId_AttackHi3, and FtMoveId_AttackLw3.
Special move IDs use cardinal suffixes: FtMoveId_SpecialN, FtMoveId_SpecialS, FtMoveId_SpecialHi, and FtMoveId_SpecialLw.
Kirby copied neutral specials use FtMoveId_KbSpecialN plus a short character code, e.g. Mr, Fx, Ca, Dk, Kp, Lk, Sk, Ns, Pe, Pp, Pk, Ss, Ys, Pr, Mt, Lg, Ms, Zd, Cl, Dr, Fc, Pc, Gw, Gn, Fe, Gk.
Item swing move IDs include item name plus phase/swing type suffixes, e.g. SwordSwing1, SwordSwing3, SwordSwing4, SwordSwingDash.
Motion flag constants use the ftCo_MF_ or per-character ftXX_MF_ prefix and now have type MotionFlags.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Kept the packed move ID expression as a compile-time constant but changed multiplication by 1 << 24 into left shift by 24, a clearer equivalent form for high-byte packing.
Preserved ATTRIBUTE_USED on static flag constants, suggesting the constants were kept as named symbols or to avoid removal despite being header-defined.
Used a dedicated symbol replacement tool, melee-replace-symbols, for bulk renames instead of ad hoc sed escaping in tools/functions.sh.
No explicit assembly-matching discussion is present in the PR text or review comments; matching impact is inferred only from the mechanical constant-expression and typedef changes in the diff.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
Because there is no PR body or reviewer discussion, reviewer intent and any requested corrections cannot be determined from the evidence.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename remaining FtMoveId_Unk48 or FtMoveId_Unk49 entries.
Search for MotionFlags typedef usage and whether MotionState struct fields were later typed more specifically.
Audit all MotionState table third fields for FtMoveId_* << 24 consistency after PR 814.
Search for melee-replace-symbols adoption in other tooling or rename-heavy PRs.
Compare ftCo_MF_* common item swing flags against per-character duplicates remaining after this PR.
